Title: Molecular Characterisation of the Peroxidase Gene Family in Botrytis cinerea and the Role of BcPRD7 in Virulence

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcHEX was identified from ΔBcPRD7-related analyses as a Woronin body protein likely linked to cell integrity. 2. BcHEX physically interacted with BcPRD7 by yeast two-hybrid, luciferase complementation, and Co-IP. 3. Expression of BcHEX in wild-type B05.10 increased total peroxidase activity by about 20%, and expression in ΔBcPRD7 increased activity by about 30%, indicating BcHEX promotes BcPRD7-associated peroxidase activity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; luciferase complementation; Co-IP; transformation into B05.10 and ΔBcPRD7 followed by peroxidase activity assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Identified from RNA-seq/GO-KEGG interpretation of ΔBcPRD7 background; accession not reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not an enzyme gene or TF; proposed to affect Woronin body function and thereby fungal cell integrity and peroxidase-associated virulence traits.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Oxidative homeostasis linked to cell integrity and pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes peroxidase activity and is implicated in cell integrity/virulence through interaction with BcPRD7
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host infection context; peroxidase activity assays in transformed strains
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal intervention target affecting Woronin body integrity and virulence-related oxidative functions.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD3 expression was strongly induced by H2O2 stress and also upregulated during hyphal development, Arabidopsis thaliana infection, and early conidial development. 2. ΔBcPRD3 mutants showed thinner and longer hyphae, sharply reduced conidial production and size, delayed and smaller infection cushions, altered stress responses, and reduced virulence. 3. Knockout reduced intracellular ROS level and downregulated several cell-wall-degrading enzyme genes, supporting a positive role in oxidative homeostasis and pathogenic development.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing B. cinerea virulence by impairing oxidative stress management and infection development; not a crop nutrient-improvement gene.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene was one of four experimentally prioritized peroxidase genes from the family-wide survey.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ROS scavenging / fungal oxidative stress response
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profile data of hyphal and conidial development downloaded from GEO accession not reported
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for scavenging/modulation of target ROS pool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High under H2O2 stress; also elevated during hyphal development, infection, and at 4 h conidial stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes strong developmental defects and reduced virulence
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Peroxidase-mediated ROS detoxification / oxidative stress metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression profiling; mutant analysis; stress assays; virulence assay; ROS measurement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Oxidative homeostasis affecting fungal development and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates ROS homeostasis, growth, conidiation, infection-structure formation, stress adaptation, and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High under H2O2 stress; also elevated during hyphal development, infection, and at 4 h conidial stage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses hydrogen peroxide or related peroxides to oxidize electron-donor substrates and contribute to peroxide scavenging

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD7 was strongly induced by H2O2 stress and during infection-related development. 2. ΔBcPRD7 caused thin elongated hyphae, strongly reduced conidiation, smaller and fewer infection cushions, altered stress responses, and almost complete loss of lesion formation on Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Recombinant BcPRD7 showed direct peroxidase activity, and ΔBcPRD7 had reduced intracellular ROS and lower total peroxidase activity. 4. RNA-seq of ΔBcPRD7 indicated changes in oxidoreductase activity, ion transport, mycotoxin synthesis/metabolism, and carbohydrate-related pathways. 5. BcPRD7 physically interacted with BcHEX, and BcHEX expression increased peroxidase activity in wild type and ΔBcPRD7 backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate antifungal target because its disruption nearly abolishes virulence and compromises oxidative metabolism; relevant for disease control rather than crop nutrient enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most deeply characterized core gene in the paper; proposed to function via Woronin body-related processes through interaction with BcHEX.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for fungal virulence-associated peroxidase activity, but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ROS scavenging / fungal oxidative stress response
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq comparing B05.10 and ΔBcPRD7; accession not reported; developmental expression data from GEO accession not reported
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for scavenging/modulation of target ROS pool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High under H2O2 stress; also induced during hyphal development, infection, and early conidial development
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes major growth, sporulation, infection-structure, stress-response, and virulence defects
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Peroxidase-mediated ROS detoxification / oxidative stress metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout; complementation; recombinant protein enzyme assay; ROS measurement; RNA-seq; Y2H; luciferase complementation; Co-IP; peroxidase activity assay in BcHEX transformants; virulence assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Oxidative homeostasis affecting fungal development, cell integrity, and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes peroxidase activity, oxidative homeostasis, growth and development, stress adaptation, and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High under H2O2 stress; also induced during hyphal development, infection, and early conidial development
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a peroxidase that consumes hydrogen peroxide or related peroxides while oxidizing donor substrates, contributing to ROS scavenging

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD8 was induced by H2O2 and showed elevated expression during hyphal development, infection, and early conidial development. 2. ΔBcPRD8 mutants displayed thinner and longer hyphae, reduced conidiation and conidial size, delayed/smaller infection structures, increased sensitivity to several stresses, and reduced virulence on Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Knockout also reduced intracellular ROS level and lowered expression of several cell-wall-degrading enzyme genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential fungicide or host-induced gene silencing target to suppress B. cinerea pathogenic fitness.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as one of four focal peroxidase genes prioritized from the family survey.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Developmental expression data from GEO accession not reported
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for scavenging/modulation of target ROS pool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High under H2O2 stress; also elevated during hyphal development, infection, and at 4 h conidial stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes major developmental and virulence defects
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Peroxidase-mediated ROS detoxification / oxidative stress metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression profiling; mutant analysis; stress assays; virulence assay; ROS measurement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Oxidative homeostasis affecting fungal development and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates ROS homeostasis, growth, conidiation, infection-structure formation, stress adaptation, and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High under H2O2 stress; also elevated during hyphal development, infection, and at 4 h conidial stage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses hydrogen peroxide or related peroxides to oxidize electron-donor substrates and contribute to peroxide scavenging

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD10 expression increased under H2O2 stress and during developmental/infection stages. 2. ΔBcPRD10 mutants had thinner elongated hyphae, fewer and smaller conidia, delayed and fewer infection cushions, altered stress responses, and reduced virulence on Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Knockout lowered intracellular ROS level and reduced expression of several cell-wall-degrading enzyme genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential disease-control target for weakening B. cinerea oxidative metabolism and virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although BcPRD10 lacks the Dyp assignment of BcPRD3/7/8, it was one of the four primary experimentally validated family genes.

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes developmental and virulence defects
